message:
Improve verify command output with skip tracking

- Track skipped agents when no backup exists
- Show named agent in warning instead of raw ID
- Update summary to distinguish between failed and not-found cases
- Fix test scripts for updated verify behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2770,7 +2770,9 @@ verify_all() {
         local dest
         dest=$(get_agent_backup_dir "$agent")
         if [[ ! -d "$dest" ]]; then
-            log_debug "Skipping $agent: no backup exists"
+            local agent_name="${AGENT_NAMES[$agent]:-$agent}"
+            log_warn "${agent_name}: No backup found"
+            ((skipped++))
             continue
         fi
 
@@ -2784,10 +2786,12 @@ verify_all() {
     # Summary
     printf '\n' >&2
     printf '%s\n' "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────" >&2
-    if [[ "$failed" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    if [[ "$failed" -eq 0 && "$skipped" -eq 0 ]]; then
         log_success "All backups verified: ${verified} OK"
+    elif [[ "$failed" -eq 0 ]]; then
+        log_warn "Verification complete: ${verified} OK, ${skipped} not found"
     else
-        log_warn "Verification complete: ${verified} OK, ${failed} failed"
+        log_warn "Verification complete: ${verified} OK, ${failed} failed, ${skipped} not found"
     fi
 
     [[ "$failed" -eq 0 ]]

@@ -184,9 +184,9 @@ test_concurrent_export() {
     local export2="${TEST_ENV_ROOT}/export2.tar.gz"
 
     # Export same agent to different files
-    run_asb export claude -o "$export1" &
+    run_asb export claude "$export1" &
     local pid1=$!
-    run_asb export claude -o "$export2" &
+    run_asb export claude "$export2" &
     local pid2=$!
 
     wait $pid1

@@ -221,7 +221,7 @@ test_export_with_symlinks() {
     assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
 
     local export_file="${TEST_ENV_ROOT}/export_symlink.tar.gz"
-    run_asb export claude -o "$export_file"
+    run_asb export claude "$export_file"
     assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
 
     assert_file_exists "$export_file" || return 1
@@ -244,7 +244,7 @@ test_import_with_symlinks() {
     assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
 
     local export_file="${TEST_ENV_ROOT}/export_symlink2.tar.gz"
-    run_asb export claude -o "$export_file"
+    run_asb export claude "$export_file"
     assert_exit_code 0 "$ASB_LAST_STATUS"
 
     # Clear and reimport
